degraded: Small standalone follow-up to G3 (CTA copy + one static link). Internal opus code review = NO blocking issues; of its 2 low advisories, #1 (uniform attribute-value seam: route _attrs through the same strip_terminal+escape as text nodes) was applied + pinned by a test, #2 (double-quote-coupled regex) is redundant with the authoritative parser-based test. External LLM review skipped (advisory at small complexity).

behaviors.behavior: CTA explains what /shipwright-adopt does (3 steps) + the concrete next step; 'certify' removed from the CTA (stays only in the honest-ceiling disclaimer)
The CTA is a single static, trusted, hardened link to svenroth.ai/shipwright (target=_blank, rel=noopener noreferrer) — the only <a> in the document
Security invariant TIGHTENED not loosened: no auto-fetch surface; exactly one href = the CTA even under hostile input; a repo-derived string never becomes an <a> or href
Attribute values share the text-node cleaning seam (control/ANSI/bidi stripped, quotes/brackets escaped) so an attribute is never a weaker context than a text node
The CLI --format html path emits only the CTA link (no other external reference)
HTML golden snapshot regenerated for the new CTA and byte-exact enforced; render stays deterministic
